reasoning: Artist and release name are strong identifiers with possible formatting differences, so we use character-based matching for artist and token-based cosine for title; track name lists are compared as sets via Jaccard to capture overlapping tracklists; duration provides a weak numeric consistency check. Weights emphasize the three textual identifiers, and the threshold is set moderately high to favor precision while allowing for minor variations.

Use title (name) and artist as primary identifiers with normalization, include release-date within 1 year to align editions, exploit track list names as a strong set-based signal for albums/singles, and use total duration as a soft consistency check. Weights emphasize name and artist while keeping date, tracks, and duration contributory but secondary, with a moderately high threshold for good precision in ml mode.

Use title/name as primary signal (cosine on lowercased strings), artist as a near-identifier with typo-tolerant matching, track-name lists compared via set-based cosine/Jaccard for multi-track releases, and duration as a weak numeric consistency check. Slightly higher threshold balances precision/recall for ML mode given strong textual identifiers.
